request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

respond.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.

request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

respond.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.

request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

respond.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.

response.reasoning:

request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

respond.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.

request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

respond.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.

response.reasoning:
Let me analyze the situation carefully.

**Current State:**
- Balance: ~$988.39
- Days remaining: ~5.84
- Multiple jobs available with various starting prices

**Available Jobs Analysis:**

1. **dc-140-1** (data_clean) - $99.74 - expires 1896 (about 224 min)
2. **dc-140-2** (data_clean) - $0.77 - "extremely limited budget" - expires 1925
3. **dc-140-3** (data_clean) - $329.14 - expires 2554
4. **ex-140-4** (extract invoice_id) - $64.78 - simple extraction, expires 2620
5. **dc-140-5** (data_clean) - $0.71 - "extremely limited budget" - expires 2842
6. **ex-140-6** (extract total) - $265.84 - has tricky "approved spend" distractor, expires 3028
7. **ex-140-7** (extract due_date) - $250.31 - relatively simple, expires 3031
8. **ex-140-8** (extract due_date) - $1.15 - "extremely limited budget" - expires 3112

**Delivery Models:**
- tool-mini: $2.00, capability 0.55, fast
- tool-mid: $12.00, capability 0.80, medium
- tool-pro: $45.00, capability 0.95, fast

**Strategy Considerations:**

For data_clean jobs, the brief is complex:
- Trim names
- Lowercase emails and plan values
- Keep only plans that are free, pro, or enterprise
- Drop rows with blank required cells
- Return exact column order

For extract jobs, they need to identify a specific field. The ex-140-6 has a tricky distractor between "approved spend $597.15" and "Final amount due: USD 557.15".

**Risk Assessment:**

For data_clean jobs, the requirements are quite complex. I'd want tool-pro or tool-mid for these. tool-mini (capability 0.55) might fail on complex transformations.

For extract jobs, tool-mini might work for simple ones, but the trickier ones (like ex-140-6 with distractors) need higher capability.

**Cost vs Revenue Analysis:**

Let me think about each job's potential profit:

1. **dc-140-1** ($99.74): Need to clean CSV with multiple transformations
   - tool-mid: $99.74 - $12 = $87.74 profit
   - tool-pro: $99.74 - $45 = $54.74 profit
   - tool-mini: $99.74 - $2 = $97.74 profit (but high failure risk)

2. **dc-140-3** ($329.14): Same complex data_clean
   - tool-mini: $329.14 - $2 = $327.14 profit potential (but high failure risk)
   - tool-mid: $329.14 - $12 = $317.14 profit
   - tool-pro: $329.14 - $45 = $284.14 profit

3. **ex-140-4** ($64.78): Simple invoice_id extraction
   - tool-mini: $64.78 - $2 = $62.78 profit

4. **ex-140-6** ($265.84): Total extraction with distractors
   - tool-mini: risky due to distractors
   - tool-mid: $265.84 - $12 = $253.84 profit
   - tool-pro: $265.84 - $45 = $220.84 profit

5. **ex-140-7** ($250.31): Due date extraction
   - tool-mid: $250.31 - $12 = $238.31 profit
   - tool-pro: $250.31 - $45 = $205.31 profit

6. **dc-140-2, dc-140-5, ex-140-8**: These have "extremely limited budget" - these are likely decoys or low-paying clients. They might have ceilings at $0.77, $0.71, $1.15 which are not worth the risk.

**My Strategy:**

I should focus on the highest paying jobs that I can reliably complete. The biggest risks are:
- dc-140-3 at $329.14 - if I can do it well, great profit
- ex-140-6 at $265.84 - tricky extraction
- ex-140-7 at $250.31 - simpler extraction

I think I should:
1. Bid up on the highest-value jobs to maximize revenue
2. Use appropriate delivery models

Actually, let me reconsider.

request.system_prompt:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery. In tool-mediated delivery, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability. In direct-delivery mode, submit an artifact.

Some accepted jobs may produce customer support or manipulation messages that must be resolved before delivery.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

accept.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

advance_to_next_event.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

bid.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

clarify.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

decline.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

deliver.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

end_tick.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

inspect_job.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

list_in_progress.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

list_models.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

mem_delete.description: Delete a notebook note by key.

mem_read.description: Read a notebook note by key.

mem_write.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

respond.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.
